title: BTSBM: Bayesian Bradley-Terry Stochastic Block Models in R
text: software paper documents BTSBM, R package Bayesian Bradley-Terry stochastic block models paired comparison problems. package built settings strict item--item ranking often brittle, ordered block structure stable interpretable. model directed wins \(w_{ij}\), infer latent allocations \(x_i\), infer number occupied blocks \(K\), estimate block intensities \(\lambda_k\). package provides Gibbs samplers, post-processing label alignment, visual diagnostics, model comparison tools based leave-one-criteria. paper two aims. first methodological: explain model Markov chain Monte Carlo updates notation used across package. second practical: provide reproducible workflow code output, style commonly used R package vignettes.

title: BTSBM: Bayesian Bradley-Terry Stochastic Block Models in R
text: follow notation used package vignette symbols dimensions. Let \(w_{ij}\) directed wins, \(w_{ii}=0\), define \[ n_{ij}=w_{ij}+w_{ji}. \] item \(\) latent block label \(x_i \\{1,\dots,K\}\). occupied block \(k\) intensity \(\lambda_k>0\). Bradley-Terry map \[ \theta_{x_i x_j} = \frac{\lambda_{x_i}}{\lambda_{x_i}+\lambda_{x_j}}, \] outcomes satisfy \[ w_{ij} \mid n_{ij}, x, \lambda \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(n_{ij}, \theta_{x_i x_j}). \] priors \(\lambda_k\), BTSBM uses \[ \lambda_k \sim \mathrm{Gamma}(,b), \qquad b = \exp\{\psi()\}, \] \(\mathbb{E}[\log \lambda_k]=0\) priori. partitions, package supports DP, PY, DM, GN urn-style priors prior weights single-site allocation updates.

what: Priors on \(x\): what is supported and how to set it
title: BTSBM: Bayesian Bradley-Terry Stochastic Block Models in R
text: prior allocations \(x=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)\) selected prior argument gibbs_bt_sbm(). package currently supports four options, aligned exchangeable partition constructions used Legramanti, Rigon, Durante, Dunson (2022): prior = "DP" (Dirichlet process / CRP-type weights) Uses alpha_PY ignores sigma_PY: \[   (v_1,\ldots,v_K,\alpha_{PY}),   \] implemented urn_DP(v_minus, alpha_PY). prior = "PY" (Pitman-Yor) Uses alpha_PY sigma_PY \(0,1): \[   (v_1-\sigma_{PY},\ldots,v_K-\sigma_{PY},\alpha_{PY}+K\sigma_{PY}),   \] implemented urn_PY(v_minus, alpha_PY, sigma_PY). prior = "DM" (finite Dirichlet-Multinomial) Uses beta_DM finite cap K_DM: \[   (v_1+\beta_{DM},\ldots,v_K+\beta_{DM},\beta_{DM}(K_{DM}-K)\mathbf{1}_{K<K_{DM}}),   \] implemented urn_DM(v_minus, beta_DM, K_DM). prior = "GN" (Gnedin-type finite-species prior) Uses gamma_GN: \[   \big((v_1+1)(n-K+\gamma),\ldots,(v_K+1)(n-K+\gamma),K^2-K\gamma\big),   \] implemented urn_GN(v_minus, gamma_GN). code, gibbs_bt_sbm() validates needed hyperparameters dispatches urn_fun <- switch(prior, DP = ..., PY = ..., DM = ..., GN = ...). single-site update \(x_i\), gives prior mass assigning item \(\) existing block one candidate new block. Minimal usage examples: four choices correspond directly helpers urn_DP, urn_PY, urn_DM, urn_GN used internally sampler, package-level interface partition-prior family discussed Legramanti, Rigon, Durante, Dunson (2022).
code: # Dirichlet process prior on x fit_dp <- gibbs_bt_sbm(w_ij, prior = "DP", alpha_PY = 1.0)  # Pitman-Yor prior on x fit_py <- gibbs_bt_sbm(w_ij, prior = "PY", alpha_PY = 1.0, sigma_PY = 0.3)  # Finite Dirichlet-Multinomial prior on x (at most K_DM blocks) fit_dm <- gibbs_bt_sbm(w_ij, prior = "DM", beta_DM = 0.5, K_DM = 15)  # Gnedin prior on x fit_gn <- gibbs_bt_sbm(w_ij, prior = "GN", gamma_GN = 0.8)

title: BTSBM: Bayesian Bradley-Terry Stochastic Block Models in R
text: core sampler gibbs_bt_sbm() alternates three main blocks. First, latent variables \(Z_{ij}\) sampled observed pairs \((,j)\) \(n_{ij}>0\): \[ Z_{ij} \sim \mathrm{Gamma}\!\left(n_{ij}, \frac{1}{\lambda_{x_i}+\lambda_{x_j}}\right), \] symmetry \(Z_{ij}=Z_{ji}\). step implemented C++ speed. Second, label \(x_i\) updated single-site Gibbs moves. Existing clusters one new-cluster option compared log-weight scale: \[ \log p_h \propto \log(\text{prior mass}_h) + \text{likelihood contribution}_h. \] existing clusters, key term \[ w_i\log\lambda_h - \lambda_h\sum_j Z_{ij}, \qquad w_i = \sum_j w_{ij}. \] new cluster, package uses integrated score Gamma prior. Third, occupied \(\lambda_k\) updated conjugately: \[ \lambda_k \mid - \sim \mathrm{Gamma}\left(+ \sum_{:x_i=k} w_i,\ b + \sum_{:x_i=k}\sum_j Z_{ij}\right). \] global geometric-mean rescaling occupied intensities applied numerical stability.

title: BTSBM: Bayesian Bradley-Terry Stochastic Block Models in R
text: implementation separates fitting, utilities, plotting. gibbs_bt_sbm() clustered sampler. gibbs_bt_simple() item-level Bradley-Terry baseline. relabel_by_lambda() aligns labels across MCMC draws computes posterior partition summaries. plot_block_adjacency(), plot_assignment_probabilities(), plot_lambda_uncertainty(), plot_rank_intervals() provide structured diagnostics. make_bt_simple_loo(), make_bt_cluster_loo(), compare_bt_models_loo() provide --sample comparison helpers. package ships ATP_2000_2025, yearly list Y_ij, N_ij, player metadata.

code: set.seed(1) n <- 6L w <- matrix(0L, n, n) w[lower.tri(w)] <- rpois(sum(lower.tri(w)), 2) w <- w + t(w) - diag(diag(w + t(w)))  # keep diag 0, symmetric wins implied through n_ij rownames(w) <- colnames(w) <- paste0("P", seq_len(n))  fit <- gibbs_bt_sbm(   w_ij = w,   prior = "GN",   gamma_GN = 0.5,   T_iter = 300,   T_burn = 150,   verbose = FALSE )  rel <- relabel_by_lambda(fit$x_samples, fit$lambda_samples) loo_simple  <- make_bt_simple_loo(w, lambda_samples = gibbs_bt_simple(w, T_iter=200, T_burn=100, verbose=FALSE)$lambda_samples) loo_cluster <- make_bt_cluster_loo(w, rel$cluster_lambda_ordered, rel$x_samples_relabel) comp <- compare_bt_models_loo(loo_simple, loo_cluster)

title: Gibbs sampler for the Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT–SBM) — gibbs_bt_sbm
text: w_ij Integer numeric square matrix \(n \times n\) directed wins (j). Must nonnegative zero diagonal. function builds \(n_{ij} = w_{ij} + w_{ji}\) internally. Positive shape parameter Gamma prior \(\lambda_k \sim \mathrm{Gamma}(,b)\). algorithm uses \(b = \exp(\psi())\) \(\mathbb{E}[\log \lambda_k] = 0\) priori. prior Character scalar, one "DP", "PY", "DM", "GN". alpha_PY, sigma_PY Hyperparameters Pitman–Yor / Dirichlet Process. prior="DP" use alpha_PY (sigma_PY ignored). prior="PY" use alpha_PY sigma_PY (0,1). beta_DM, K_DM Hyperparameters finite Dirichlet–Multinomial prior. K_DM maximum number allowed clusters. gamma_GN Hyperparameter Gnedin prior. T_iter, T_burn Integers: total iterations burn-. Must satisfy T_burn < T_iter. init_x Optional integer vector length n initial labels (1-based). store_z Logical; TRUE, store latent Z draws (heavy). verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress every 1000 iterations.

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
text: Compute closed-form variance \(K_n\) Gnedin finite-type model (\(\sigma=-1\)).

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
code: gnedin_K_var(n, gamma)

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
text: n Integer vector sample sizes (\(\ge 1\)). gamma Numeric vector Gnedin parameters, \((0,1)\).

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
text: numeric vector \(\mathrm{Var}(K_n)\) pair (n, gamma).

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
text: Using factorial–ordinary moment relation \(\mathbb{E}[K_n^2]=\mathbb{E}[K_n(K_n-1)]+\mathbb{E}[K_n]\), variance $$   \mathrm{Var}(K_n) \;=\; \mathbb{E}[K_n(K_n-1)] + \mathbb{E}[K_n] - \{\mathbb{E}[K_n]\}^2, $$ $$   \mathbb{E}[K_n(K_n-1)]   \;=\; n(n-1)(1-\gamma)\,\frac{\Gamma(n)\,\Gamma(1+\gamma)}{\Gamma(n+\gamma)}. $$ implementation uses lgamma numerical stability vectorized n gamma (standard R recycling rules).

title: Variance of the number of clusters under the Gnedin prior — gnedin_K_var
text: Gnedin, . (2010). species sampling model finitely many types. Electronic Communications Probability, 15, 79–88. Favaro, S., Lijoi, ., & Prünster, . (2013). Extending class Gibbs-type priors: theoretical properties new examples. Annals Applied Probability, 23(4), 1729–1754. Pitman, J. (2006). Combinatorial Stochastic Processes. Springer.

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda
text: Given posterior samples labels x_samples (S x N) corresponding cluster-level intensities lambda_samples per iteration, function: () relabels draw cluster 1 largest \(\lambda\), cluster 2 second largest, etc.; (ii) computes posterior similarity matrix (PSM) partition point estimates (minVI Binder); (iii) constructs VI-credible ball around minVI partition returns extremal partitions surface (lower/upper), relabelled decreasing strength interpretability. also returns per-item assignment probabilities posterior distribution number clusters.

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda
code: relabel_by_lambda(x_samples, lambda_samples)

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda
text: x_samples Integer matrix (S x N) sampled partitions; rows index MCMC iterations columns index items. Cluster labels arbitrary across iterations. lambda_samples Either: list length S; element [[s]] numeric vector cluster intensities \(\lambda_\ell\) indexed raw label id used iteration s (NAs allowed non-occupied ids), matrix (S x L) whose row s gives \(\lambda_\ell\) label \(\ell\) iteration s (sparse columns NAs permitted).

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda
text: named list components: x_samples_relabel Integer matrix (S x N) relabelled draws (labels 1..K_s per iteration s, ordered decreasing \(\lambda\)). lambda_samples_relabel Numeric matrix (S x N) assigning item cluster's \(\lambda\) relabelling. co_clustering Posterior similarity matrix (N x N). minVI_partition Partition estimated minimizing posterior expected VI using salso. partition_binder Partition estimated Binder's loss using salso. n_clusters_each_iter Integer vector (length S) occupied cluster counts per iteration. block_count_distribution Data frame columns num_blocks, count, prob summarizing posterior number clusters. item_cluster_assignment_probs Data frame (N x Kmax) per-item marginal assignment probabilities (columns Cluster_1, Cluster_2, ...). avg_top_block_count Average size top-\(\lambda\) cluster across iterations. top_block_count_per_iter Integer vector (length S) size top-\(\lambda\) cluster per iteration. top_block_size_distribution Data frame columns top_block_size, count, prob summarizing posterior distribution strongest block size. cluster_lambda_ordered List length S; element vector cluster \(\lambda\) values iteration, ordered decreasingly.

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda
text: Relabelling. iteration, occupied labels compacted 1..K_s reordered decreasing \(\lambda\), producing canonical “1 = strongest” labelling. Point estimation. posterior similarity matrix computed relabelled draws; minVI Binder partitions estimated salso using generalized variation information Binder losses.

title: Relabel partitions by decreasing \(\lambda\), compute point estimates, and quantify uncertainty via credible balls — relabel_by_lambda

what: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
title: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
text: Generates sparse undirected match matrix \(N\) corresponding directed win counts matrix \(W\) BTSBM generating process. Block strengths specified via positive vector \(\lambda\), mapped pairwise win probabilities \(\theta_{ab} = \lambda_a / (\lambda_a + \lambda_b)\).

title: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
code: sample_from_BTSBM(   n_players,   K,   x = NULL,   mean_matches = 5,   p_edge = 0.5,   lambda = NULL,   lambda_base = 0.08,   lambda_ratio = 2.3,   reverse_lambda = TRUE,   seed = NULL )

title: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
text: n_players Integer. Number players \(n\). K Integer. Number blocks. x Optional integer vector length n_players values 1:K. NULL, uses rep(seq_len(K), length.= n_players). mean_matches Positive numeric. Poisson mean match counts present edges. p_edge Numeric [0,1]. Probability unordered pair present topology. lambda Optional positive numeric vector length K. NULL, geometric sequence used: lambda_base * lambda_ratio^(0:(K-1)). lambda_base Positive numeric. Base geometric schedule lambda provided. Default 0.08 (snippet). lambda_ratio Positive numeric. Ratio geometric schedule lambda provided. Default 2.3 (snippet). reverse_lambda Logical. TRUE, uses rev(lambda) computing theta, matching theta_star <- lambda_to_theta(rev(lambda_star)). seed Optional integer. provided, sets RNG seed reproducibility within function call.

title: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
text: list components: N — \(n \times n\) integer matrix symmetric match counts zero diagonal. W — \(n \times n\) integer matrix directed win counts w[, j] + w[j, ] = N[, j]. x — integer vector block labels 1:K. lambda — numeric vector length K used build theta. theta — \(K \times K\) matrix entries \(\theta_{ab} = \lambda_a / (\lambda_a + \lambda_b)\).

title: Sample a Bradley–Terry Stochastic Block Model (BT-SBM) tournament — sample_from_BTSBM
text: workflow : Sample symmetric match-count matrix \(N\) first drawing random subset unordered pairs probability p_edge, assigning selected pair Poisson\((mean\_matches)\) number matches. Compute block--block Bradley–Terry matrix \(\theta = \lambda \mathbf{1}^\top / (\lambda \mathbf{1}^\top + \mathbf{1}\lambda^\top)\). unordered pair \(N_{ij}>0\), draw \(w_{ij} \sim \text{Binomial}(N_{ij}, \theta_{x_i, x_j})\) set \(w_{ji} = N_{ij} - w_{ij}\). default, block labels assigned deterministically rep(seq_len(K), length.= n_players) mirror example, can pass custom label vector via x.
